title: Markers of Development (Yahir's Travel Heuristics)
html:
Yahir's personal checklist for ranking how "advanced" a country feels, drafted from inside the china chapter (2024 08 31 determinants of advanced countries). Mixes infrastructure questions (flushable toilet paper, AC, hand-drying, card acceptance) with social/cultural ones (English availability, kid behavior, women's style, lifting culture, museum density, airport security). Sibling to non negotiables (preparation framework) — markers-of-development is the observation framework.

TL;DR

  • 13 inbound citations — formalized August 2024 mid-China but the disposition predates the trip.
  • Origin: 2024 08 31 determinants of advanced countries (also captured as determinants of advanced countries checklist).
  • The list is explicitly opinionated and personal — not "objective development indicators," it's Yahir's lived-experience proxy set.
  • Categories span: infrastructure (toilets, AC, payments), social (kids, women's style, lifting), language access (English signage / staff), public goods (museums, airport security).
  • Functions in the corpus as the observational lens Yahir runs on every trip — China, Vietnam, Egypt, Jordan, Israel, India, Mexico, Brazil all get scored implicitly.
  • Pre-trip planning version is non negotiables; mid-trip observation is markers-of-development; post-trip integration is end of chapter reflection.

Key markers (from the vault)

Infrastructure layer:

  • Flushable toilet paper (vs septic-restricted)
  • Air conditioning ubiquity
  • Hand-drying provisions
  • Card / contactless payment acceptance
  • AliPay / WeChat-style super-app density

Social layer:

  • English availability (signage, transit, customer service)
  • Kid behavior in public (calm, helmeted, supervised)
  • Women's style / fashion sophistication
  • Lifting culture / gym density (proxy for health-investment)
  • Museum density per capita (proxy for cultural-investment)

Trust layer:

  • Airport security calibration (over- vs under-)
  • Police visibility / civility
  • Theft baseline

Why this exists

The list is anti-stereotype scaffolding. Rather than carry preformed ideas about which countries are "developed," Yahir maintains his own observable proxy set — surprising results force him to update prior beliefs. The China chapter was the catalyst: first-world infrastructure (Alipay, high-speed rail, super-clean transit) coexisting with markers Yahir's own country (US) doesn't hit. The list lets him hold this complexity.

Source summaries

  • 2024 08 31 determinants of advanced countries — origin
  • 2024 09 02 notes on the chinese — applied to Chinese culture observations
  • 2025 05 20 side quests list — applied to future-trip planning
  • 2021 10 26 three things manifest — proto-articulation
